description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone wall base and a pitched wooden roof covered in irregular, weathered shingles. The well features a wooden crank wheel mechanism attached to a vertical support beam, which is used to lower and raise a bucket via a rope. A wooden bucket hangs from the side, and climbing vines or foliage partially cover the stone wall and roof, adding a rustic, aged appearance. The well sits on a round, cobblestone-patterned base, suggesting it is designed for outdoor placement. The structure is robust, with thick stonework and sturdy wooden beams supporting the roof and pulley system.
description: A rustic stone water well with a shingled wooden roof, crank wheel, and bucket, partially covered by climbing vines. The well sits on a cobblestone base and features weathered, natural materials.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden bucket, rope, and dense vine growth on the roof and wall.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden crank wheel, support beams, and vines climbing up the stone wall.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the stone wall and roof, with less visible vine coverage and no bucket in sight.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the crank wheel mechanism, bucket, and the well's open interior.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden roof with overlapping shingles, supported by wooden beams; no lid or slab covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Visible wooden crank wheel with rope and a wooden bucket attached, mounted on the side of the well.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, with a wooden roof supported by beams.
attribute_decorative_elements: Climbing vines or foliage on the stone wall and roof.
